REFERENCE: This reference contains numerous red flags indicating fabrication: (1) The journal name 'Journal of Gastrointestinal Motility and Microbiome' does not exist; (2) The volume number (372) and issue number (948) are impossibly high; (3) The DOI prefix (10.5634) does not correspond to a real registrant and the suffix is malformed; (4) The article topic (mitochondrial dysfunction in chronic pain) does not fit the supposed journal scope (gastrointestinal motility/microbiome); (5) The specific day-of-month in the publication date is unusual for journal citations. This reference is almost certainly fabricated or hallucinated by an AI.
authors: Sandrone, S. is a plausible author name (the real Stefano Sandrone is a neuroscientist). However, the article described is in the wrong field for his known work (neuroscience/learning, not gastroenterology). The co-author 'et al.' is unverifiable. Given the journal mismatch, authorship is suspicious.
title: The title 'Mitochondrial dysfunction signatures in patients with unexplained chronic pain syndromes' is plausible and consistent with real biomedical research. However, mitochondrial dysfunction in chronic pain syndromes is typically published in pain, neurology, or rheumatology journals, not gastroenterology journals.
journal_name: 'Journal of Gastrointestinal Motility and Microbiome' does not appear to be a real journal. A search of journal databases (Scopus, Web of Science, PubMed) reveals no such title. The real journals in this field are 'Neurogastroenterology and Motility' and the 'Journal of Gastrointestinal Motility' (which ceased publication). A journal combining 'gastrointestinal motility' with 'microbiome' in its title is not a recognized publication. This is almost certainly a fabricated journal name.
journal_volume: Volume 372 is implausibly high for a specialized gastroenterology/microbiome journal. Most journals in this field have volumes in the single or double digits per year, not in the hundreds. This strongly suggests fabrication.
journal_issue: Issue number 948 is implausibly high for a journal volume. Issues per volume typically range from 1 to 12. An issue number in the hundreds is not standard and strongly indicates fabrication.
journal_page: Pages 1052-1062 span 11 pages, which is plausible for a research article. However, given that the journal itself appears fabricated, this page range cannot be verified.
elocator: The DOI suffix 'eye-g0wi' does not follow the standard DOI format. Real DOIs typically consist of alphanumeric characters assigned by publishers, often with prefixes like 10.1038, 10.1056, 10.1093, 10.1016, etc. The pattern 'eye-g0wi' resembles randomly generated characters and is not a valid DOI suffix. DOIs are also case-insensitive but typically don't contain such arbitrary letter-number combinations.
publication_date: The date format '(2021 Jan 22)' suggests a specific publication date. However, the granularity of 'Jan 22' (day) is unusual for a print journal reference; articles are typically cited by month and year, not specific day. This further suggests the citation was artificially constructed.
doi: The DOI '10.5634/eye-g0wi' has a prefix '10.5634' which does not correspond to any known DOI registrant. Major registrants include 10.1000 (Wiley), 10.1038 (Nature), 10.1056 (NEJM), 10.1093 (Oxford), 10.1126 (Science), 10.1136 (BMJ), 10.1016 (Elsevier), etc. The suffix 'eye-g0wi' is also not a valid DOI structure. This DOI is fabricated.
pmcid: No PMCID is provided in the citation.
pmid: No PMID is provided in the citation.
